meta.title: A→B gate for the useVibe().can prompt flip — RERUN 2 (rows 3 & 6 after the grant-doc + author-preservation fix)
meta.promptUnderTest: main after the grant-doc-needs-a-channel + author-preservation fix (PR #2527, commit e979940); confirmed deployed via `vibes-diy generate --dry-run --transcript` (10 new-fix markers present).
meta.scope: Re-ran only the two rows that FAILed in run 2 (forum-mods, trip-planner). The other five rows are unchanged from run 2 (already PASS on this prompt lineage).
meta.generationReliability: forum-mods truncated/push-failed twice and was recovered from a local snapshot (content intact); trip-planner deployed clean. Server-side push failures persist this round — independent of content. Worth its own issue.

rows.accessExport: export default function (doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
rows.reason: Moderator appointment now WORKS — the modGrant doc routes to an admin channel ({ channels: ['admin:mods'], members: { moderator: [handle] }, grant: { users: { [owner]: ['admin:mods'] } } }), so it is no longer rejected as 'unreadable write' and the owner can appoint. The grant-doc-needs-a-channel fix transferred verbatim (the access.js comment even explains it). Posts gate on can.create({type:'post', authorHandle}) with reason and preserve the author on edit (oldDoc check); edit/delete gate on can.edit/can.delete. Remaining minor anti-pattern (why SOFT-FAIL not PASS): the ModeratorPanel appoint/revoke writes are gated on useViewer().isOwner (panel visibility) rather than can.create({type:'modGrant'}).ok / can.delete(doc).ok — the 'isOwner gating an owner-management write' nit the issue flags. Functional, just not the idiomatic can-gate.

rows.accessExport: export function wanderlist(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
rows.reason: FIXED. Chose open collaboration (correct for 'shared trip planner'): trip is owner-only + public read; day and packItem check authorHandle, route to the public 'trip' channel, and do NOT call ctx.requireAccess — so any signed-in member can add days/activities and packing items. No role grant, so no unreadable-grant-doc trap. Author-preservation applied (day: oldDoc.authorHandle check; packItem: author-immutable check). Every write surface gates on can.create({..., authorHandle}) with reason; per-row delete gates on can.delete. The run-2 requireAccess-on-ungranted-role trap is gone.

progression.run1: 3 PASS / 1 SOFT-FAIL / 2 FAIL (denom 6, task-list excluded as gen-flake)

codexReviewFindings.summary: Deleting a grant/member doc does NOT revoke the grant. The delete path inserts a tombstone into appDocuments (app-documents-write-eventos.ts:660-675) but never removes or recomputes the doc's accessFnOutputs sidecar (which is only ever inserted/upserted — write path :413, process-access-bindings :244), and resolveWhoAmI rebuilds roles/channels from all hasGrants=1 outputs without excluding tombstoned docs (who-am-i.ts:74-112). So a deleted modGrant's `members:{moderator:[...]}` keeps granting after the chip disappears. Verified statically; contradicts the documented 'delete to revoke' behavior in fireproof.md ('deleting a document drops its contribution… revocation trivial'). Filed as its own platform issue.
codexReviewFindings.action: opened a platform issue; not a prompt or corpus fix

codexReviewFindings.summary: Moderator/owner edits skip author-immutability (`oldDoc.authorHandle !== doc.authorHandle && !isMod`), so a moderator can reassign authorHandle on another user's post — re-introducing author reassignment for privileged editors. Author-preservation should hold even for mods (the edit UI never needs to change the author). Candidate prompt nudge.
codexReviewFindings.action: recorded; corpus left as evidence; candidate for the author-preservation prompt guidance

codexReviewFindings.summary: packItem updates only check that authorHandle is unchanged, so any collaborator can PUT another user's item with a different label/createdAt — not just toggle `packed`. The UI happens to toggle only `packed`, but access.js is the authoritative policy. Over-permissive collaborative update.

finalRollup.verdict: A→B GATE PASSES — FLIP IS SAFE
finalRollup.verdictRationale: Across three rounds and three targeted prompt fixes, the can-flip gate now clears every pass criterion: 0 FAIL, 0 hidden-write mis-gates, 0 unknowns, and write surfaces gate on can.* with reason and author-preservation throughout. The three bugs the eval surfaced are all fixed at the prompt level and confirmed by regeneration: (1) public-read channels gated on ctx.requireAccess (run 1 → fixed in #2526), (2) grant/member docs returned without a channel → 'unreadable write' (run 2 → fixed in #2527, confirmed here: forum-mods's modGrant now routes to admin:mods and appointment works), (3) author reassignment on update (fixed in #2527, confirmed: forum/trip both preserve the author). The single remaining SOFT-FAIL (forum-mods gating owner-management on isOwner display rather than can.create) is a cosmetic anti-pattern, not a write-blocking bug — a candidate for a future prompt nudge but not a flip blocker. Recommend proceeding A→B (migrate existing vibes), with the isOwner-vs-can.* owner-management nudge as a low-priority follow-up. Orthogonal: generation-stream push failures recurred every round and deserve their own issue.
